elements.name: UploadFile
elements.docstring:
A file uploaded in a request.

Define it as a *path operation function* (or dependency) parameter.

If you are using a regular `def` function, you can use the `upload_file.file`
attribute to access the raw standard Python file (blocking, not async), useful and
needed for non-async code.

Read more about it in the
[FastAPI docs for Request Files](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/request-files/).

## Example

```python
from typing import Annotated

from fastapi import FastAPI, File, UploadFile

app = FastAPI()


@app.post("/files/")
async def create_file(file: Annotated[bytes, File()]):
    return {"file_size": len(file)}


@app.post("/uploadfile/")
async def create_upload_file(file: UploadFile):
    return {"filename": file.filename}
```

attributes.name: file
attributes.type: Annotated[BinaryIO, Doc('The standard Python file object (non-async).')]

attributes.name: filename
attributes.type: Annotated[str | None, Doc('The original file name.')]

attributes.name: size
attributes.type: Annotated[int | None, Doc('The size of the file in bytes.')]

attributes.name: headers
attributes.type: Annotated[Headers, Doc('The headers of the request.')]

attributes.name: content_type
attributes.type: Annotated[str | None, Doc('The content type of the request, from the headers.')]

elements.name: DefaultPlaceholder
elements.docstring:
You shouldn't use this class directly.

It's used internally to recognize when a default value has been overwritten, even
if the overridden default value was truthy.

elements.name: Default
elements.docstring:
You shouldn't use this function directly.

It's used internally to recognize when a default value has been overwritten, even
if the overridden default value was truthy.

elements.name: write
elements.docstring:
Write some bytes to the file.

You normally wouldn't use this from a file you read in a request.

To be awaitable, compatible with async, this is run in threadpool.

args.name: data
args.type: Annotated[bytes, Doc('\n                The bytes to write to the file.\n                ')]

elements.name: read
elements.docstring:
Read some bytes from the file.

To be awaitable, compatible with async, this is run in threadpool.

args.name: size
args.type: Annotated[int, Doc('\n                The number of bytes to read from the file.\n                ')]

elements.name: seek
elements.docstring:
Move to a position in the file.

Any next read or write will be done from that position.

To be awaitable, compatible with async, this is run in threadpool.

args.name: offset
args.type: Annotated[int, Doc('\n                The position in bytes to seek to in the file.\n                ')]

elements.name: close
elements.docstring:
Close the file.

To be awaitable, compatible with async, this is run in threadpool.
